Description: Verify branding sign-in purpose tabs fields password toggle and auto-fill are visible and understandable
Test Steps: 1) Open /login 2) Review page copy 3) Use password show/hide 4) Click auto-fill
Expected Result: Page clearly identifies the app and exposes usable login controls
Actual Result: Branding and instructions were clear; login and apply-access tabs were visible; password toggle worked; auto-fill populated demo credentials

Description: Verify actionable validation for empty and malformed credentials
Test Steps: 1) Submit blank form 2) Enter bad-email and short password 3) Submit again
Expected Result: Required and malformed fields show actionable validation
Actual Result: Blank submit showed Email is required and Password is required; malformed submit showed Invalid email address and Minimum 6 characters

Description: Verify invalid but well-formed credentials are rejected
Test Steps: 1) Enter wrong@example.com / wrongpass 2) Submit
Expected Result: Access is denied with a clear error
Actual Result: Stayed on login and showed Invalid credentials

Description: Verify access-request tab role selector submit success state and back-to-login action
Test Steps: 1) Switch to Apply Access 2) Enter username email and password 3) Change role to Law Enforcement 4) Submit 5) Click Back to Login
Expected Result: Request submits shows approval-pending feedback and returns to login
Actual Result: Submission succeeded and back-to-login worked but success copy said admin will contact your email instead of the submitted address

Description: Verify login remains readable on narrow viewport
Test Steps: 1) Resize to mobile viewport 2) Inspect login screen
Expected Result: Fields and CTAs remain readable and actionable
Actual Result: Mobile login remained readable and usable

Description: Verify logged-out access to a protected route redirects safely
Test Steps: 1) Log out 2) Open /reports directly
Expected Result: Protected route redirects to login without exposing protected content
Actual Result: /reports redirected to /login and protected content did not render

Description: Verify moderator sees moderator-appropriate navigation only
Test Steps: 1) Log in with moderator demo credentials 2) Inspect sidebar
Expected Result: Moderator sees Dashboard Data Analysis Center Reports Users and Settings only
Actual Result: Moderator nav matched expectation; no admin or LE sidebar items shown

Description: Verify sidebar collapse or expand control works
Test Steps: 1) Click Collapse sidebar 2) Click Expand sidebar
Expected Result: Sidebar collapses and restores cleanly
Actual Result: Collapse and expand both worked

Test Case Title: Moderator denial handling for admin and LE routes
Description: Verify moderator cannot access /admin or /lei
Test Steps: 1) While logged in as moderator navigate to /admin 2) Navigate to /lei
Expected Result: App denies access safely with explicit feedback
Actual Result: Both URLs silently redirected to dashboard / with no access-denied message

Description: Verify logout clears auth and blocks protected routes afterward
Test Steps: 1) Click Logout 2) Reopen /reports
Expected Result: Session ends and protected routes redirect to login
Actual Result: Logout returned to /login and reopening /reports redirected back to /login

Description: Verify KPIs activity feed recent reports category breakdown top reporters and map panel render
Test Steps: 1) Log in 2) Inspect dashboard panels
Expected Result: Core panels load or fail gracefully
Actual Result: KPIs feed recent reports categories top reporters loaded; map panel rendered fallback or loading state

Description: Verify View all and All users links navigate correctly
Test Steps: 1) Click View all 2) Return 3) Click All users
Expected Result: Links navigate to Reports and Users respectively
Actual Result: View all opened Reports; All users opened Users

Description: Verify map loads or fails gracefully
Test Steps: 1) Observe dashboard map state during and after load
Expected Result: Map loads markers or shows graceful fallback
Actual Result: Map did not load Google Maps and fell back to loading or error text without breaking page

Description: Compare high-level dashboard counts against Reports and Data Analysis Center
Test Steps: 1) Compare Dashboard totals to Reports ALL REPORTS 2) Compare to DAC 30D view
Expected Result: Cross-page summary data is broadly consistent
Actual Result: Dashboard showed 47 reports in last 30 days while Reports ALL REPORTS showed 0 and DAC 30D also showed 0 reports

Description: Verify list filters and detail panes load correctly in default view
Test Steps: 1) Open Reports from dashboard 2) Inspect default ALL REPORTS state
Expected Result: Reports page shows all available reports or an accurate empty state
Actual Result: Default ALL REPORTS view showed 0 reports despite dashboard showing 47 total and rejected or verified data existing

Description: Verify status filters and search change the list
Test Steps: 1) Click REJECTED 2) Search Noise
Expected Result: Filters and search update results accurately
Actual Result: REJECTED produced 23 then 22 items after actioning; searching Noise reduced list to 2 results

Description: Verify row selection and next-report navigation
Test Steps: 1) Use Select all reports 2) Click Next report
Expected Result: Selection count updates and next-report moves detail focus
Actual Result: Select-all showed the bulk-action bar and Next report advanced the detail pane

Test Case Title: Bulk reject and escalate dialogs and results
Description: Verify bulk actions use confirmation and change state correctly
Test Steps: 1) With 2 filtered rejected rows selected run BULK REJECT through confirm 2) Reselect and run BULK ESCALATE through confirm
Expected Result: Bulk actions confirm then apply meaningful state changes
Actual Result: Bulk reject showed success but left both rows still rejected and in list; bulk escalate then removed them from the filtered rejected view

Test Case Title: Single report escalate and reject behavior
Description: Verify single-item destructive actions confirm and behave logically
Test Steps: 1) On rejected report run Escalate report through confirm 2) On next rejected report run Reject report through confirm
Expected Result: Actions confirm and only allow valid state transitions
Actual Result: Escalate succeeded and removed one rejected item; reject was allowed on an already-rejected report and showed another success entry and timeline event

Description: Verify communication mode toggle and send behavior
Test Steps: 1) Switch to PUBLIC REPLY 2) Send message 3) Switch to INTERNAL NOTE
Expected Result: Mode label or placeholder changes and sent message appears in timeline
Actual Result: Public-reply mode changed placeholder and visibility text; sent message appeared in timeline; internal-note mode switched back correctly

Test Case Title: Users page renders filters and panels
Description: Verify KPI cards filters list and detail area render
Test Steps: 1) Open Users page 2) Inspect controls and list
Expected Result: Users page renders KPIs filters list and detail placeholder or panel
Actual Result: Page rendered correctly with KPIs search role filter status buttons user list and detail area

Description: Verify search or filter updates results and selected detail stays consistent
Test Steps: 1) Search moderator 2) Set role filter Moderator 3) Click SUSPENDED to force zero results
Expected Result: List and detail pane stay synchronized with current filtered result set
Actual Result: List correctly fell to 2 then 0 users but detail pane kept showing previously selected qa_request_user even when no filtered users remained

Description: Verify invite control opens a flow or provides feedback
Test Steps: 1) Click INVITE USER
Expected Result: Invite dialog or flow opens or clear feedback appears
Actual Result: Button appeared clickable but produced no visible action or feedback

Description: Verify access-request state matches success messaging and user-state display
Test Steps: 1) Submit access request from login 2) Inspect resulting user in Users page
Expected Result: Request remains pending approval until approved by admin
Actual Result: Newly requested qa_request_user appeared immediately as ACTIVE Law Enforcement user contradicting the pending-until-approved success message

Description: Verify left-side settings buttons switch content
Test Steps: 1) Open Settings 2) Click Profile Notifications Appearance Security System
Expected Result: Each section button swaps content cleanly
Actual Result: All section buttons changed the content area correctly

Description: Verify edit mode field editing and save behavior
Test Steps: 1) Click EDIT PROFILE 2) Change display name 3) Observe header 4) Click SAVE
Expected Result: Unsaved edits stay in form until save and save persists with confirmation
Actual Result: Display name and avatar updated in the header before save; saving then showed Profile display updated

Description: Verify language timezone and date-format selectors provide persistence or feedback
Test Steps: 1) Change Language to Arabic 2) Change Timezone to UTC 3) Change Date Format to YYYY-MM-DD
Expected Result: Preference changes save explicitly or show clear confirmation
Actual Result: Values changed immediately with no save CTA or confirmation in the Profile preferences section

Description: Verify toggles save weekly digest controls and send action
Test Steps: 1) Open Notifications 2) Attempt Browser Notifications and Sound Alerts toggles 3) Click SEND DIGEST NOW
Expected Result: Controls are interactable and send action completes with clear feedback
Actual Result: Browser and Sound toggles were not manually interactable; SEND DIGEST NOW disabled controls during send then returned with no clear success toast or message

Description: Verify password visibility password validation update messaging and 2FA toggle
Test Steps: 1) Enter current new and confirm password with short new value 2) Use visibility toggle 3) Click UPDATE PASSWORD 4) Try 2FA checkbox
Expected Result: Visibility control is labeled short password is rejected clearly and 2FA toggle is usable or clearly blocked
Actual Result: Visibility toggle worked but had no accessible label text; short password showed Password must be at least 8 characters; 2FA checkbox was not manually interactable

Description: Verify danger-zone confirmation and completion feedback
Test Steps: 1) Open System 2) Click RESET TO DEFAULTS 3) Confirm RESET SETTINGS
Expected Result: Confirmation appears and reset completes or fails clearly
Actual Result: Confirmation dialog appeared and reset completed with Settings reset to defaults

Test Case Title: Analytics page load and data sanity
Description: Verify KPIs charts insights hotspots heatmap and zero or error states render coherently
Test Steps: 1) Open Data Analysis Center 2) Inspect KPIs charts and insights area
Expected Result: Analytics view renders coherent data or accurate empty-state messaging
Actual Result: Page rendered but 30D analytics showed 0 reports and no data while dashboard simultaneously reported 47 reports in last 30 days

Description: Verify period buttons update the view
Test Steps: 1) Click 7D
Expected Result: Period controls update labels and content
Actual Result: KPI header changed from 30d to 7d and chart labels updated to 7-day ranges

Description: Verify refresh regenerates AI insights or fails clearly
Test Steps: 1) Click Regenerate insights 2) Wait for completion
Expected Result: Refresh shows busy state and returns insights or a clear failure
Actual Result: Button disabled during refresh then returned generated zero-data insights with just now timestamp

Description: Verify login becomes usable quickly in local dev
Test Steps: 1) Open app from localhost 2) Observe time to first usable screen
Expected Result: Login becomes usable within a reasonable local-dev time window
Actual Result: Login screen was interactable within a few seconds and usable throughout testing

Description: Verify major interactions do not produce uncaught runtime or resource issues
Test Steps: 1) Review preserved console after login navigation map views and major flows
Expected Result: No meaningful runtime or resource errors appear during core flows
Actual Result: Console contained repeated ERR_PROXY_CONNECTION_FAILED resource errors plus React Router warnings; map panels failed to load Google Maps

Description: Verify no protected content leaks when logged out and only intended demo info is exposed
Test Steps: 1) Log out 2) Reopen protected route 3) Review login screen content
Expected Result: Protected pages stay blocked while logged out and only intended demo content appears publicly
Actual Result: Protected content stayed blocked after logout; login exposed only intended demo moderator credentials

Description: Verify logged-out access handling for / /reports /users /settings /admin /lei /data-analysis-center and one unknown route
Test Steps: 1) Open each route directly while logged out in isolated tabs 2) Observe final URL and rendered screen
Expected Result: Protected and unknown routes do not expose protected content and redirect to login
Actual Result: All tested routes redirected to http://localhost:5174/login and no protected content rendered

Description: Verify N E and R work when focus is outside text inputs
Test Steps: 1) Log in as moderator 2) Open /reports 3) Switch to CLOSED 4) Click non-input content 5) Press N then E and cancel then R and cancel
Expected Result: N selects next report E opens escalate flow and R opens reject flow
Actual Result: N advanced selection; E opened ESCALATE THIS REPORT modal; R opened REJECT THIS REPORT modal

Description: Verify the report list or detail splitter can be dragged and layout stays usable
Test Steps: 1) Log in as moderator 2) Open /reports 3) Capture splitter widths 4) Drag queue/detail separator to the right 5) Re-measure widths
Expected Result: Splitter moves and both panels remain usable
Actual Result: Splitter moved successfully; list width changed from 380px to 504px and detail width from 524px to 440px; page remained usable

Test Case Title: Report Open in Maps link validity
Description: Verify report detail exposes a valid external maps URL
Test Steps: 1) Log in as moderator 2) Open /reports 3) Select a report with location 4) Inspect and open Open in Maps
Expected Result: Link resolves to a valid external maps URL with coordinates
Actual Result: Link href was a valid Google Maps query URL; external open could not complete in this environment because browser traffic to Google Maps failed with ERR_PROXY_CONNECTION_FAILED

Description: Verify 90D and 1Y update analytics views and expose internally consistent populated states
Test Steps: 1) Log in as moderator 2) Open /data-analysis-center 3) Click 90D and record KPI and trend changes 4) Click 1Y and record changes again
Expected Result: Period label KPIs charts and supporting sections update to the selected range
Actual Result: 90D updated to populated analytics with 31 reports; 1Y updated to populated analytics with 47 reports and visible weekly trend hotspot and category data

Description: Determine whether previously hard-to-target checkbox or toggle controls are broken or only difficult to interact with
Test Steps: 1) Log in as moderator 2) Open Settings > Notifications 3) Attempt Browser Notifications Sound Alerts and Weekly Digest toggles 4) Log in as LE 5) Test Publish to Community Feed and Fuzz Location for Privacy toggles in LE detail
Expected Result: Reachable toggles change state when activated and non-interactive controls clearly indicate disabled state
Actual Result: In Settings Browser Notifications and Sound Alerts could not be activated by normal click and did not change even when their underlying inputs were triggered directly; Weekly Digest toggled successfully; in LE detail Publish toggled on and enabled Fuzz which also toggled successfully

Test Case Title: Newly created law-enforcement account login attempt
Description: Verify whether the newly created law-enforcement account from prior access-request flow can authenticate
Test Steps: 1) Confirm the account exists in /users as qa_request_user / qa.request@example.com 2) Log out in a separate session 3) Attempt sign-in with qa.request@example.com and the prior test password candidate 4) Inspect login feedback
Expected Result: If the account is active and login-enabled authentication succeeds or fails with state consistent with Users Management
Actual Result: Login was blocked with Account pending admin approval while the same account appears in /users as LE and ACTIVE

Test Case Title: Built-in LE login /lei access and sidebar visibility
Description: Verify reachable LE login and LE-role navigation using a login that succeeds
Test Steps: 1) In a fresh session sign in with lei@safesignal.com / password123 2) Open /lei 3) Inspect shell label and visible sidebar links
Expected Result: LE login succeeds /lei is accessible and sidebar or shell reflects LE role with LE-appropriate navigation only
Actual Result: Login succeeded and /lei was accessible but the shell still displayed Moderator Dashboard; sidebar also exposed Dashboard and Data Analysis Center in addition to LE Interface and Settings

Test Case Title: LE queue search filter sort and splitter
Description: Verify LE queue search/filter behavior list consistency and queue/detail splitter
Test Steps: 1) Log in as LE 2) Open /lei 3) Search Shooting 4) Clear search 5) Switch status filter from Pending Action to Dispatched and All Active 6) Click sort 7) Drag queue/detail splitter
Expected Result: Search and filters show matching incidents only All Active excludes closed cases detail stays in sync with queue results and splitter resizes cleanly
Actual Result: Search for Shooting narrowed the queue correctly and splitter worked from 420 to 594; however Dispatched plus search produced NO INCIDENTS FOUND while stale incident detail remained visible and All Active displayed many CLOSED incidents in the queue

Description: Verify OPERATIONS MAP and CLOSED CASES tabs switch views cleanly
Test Steps: 1) Log in as LE 2) Open /lei 3) Click OPERATIONS MAP 4) Observe map tab content 5) Click CLOSED CASES 6) Wait for data load
Expected Result: Both alternate tabs switch cleanly and load their own content
Actual Result: OPERATIONS MAP switched to map view and showed ALL ACTIVE INCIDENTS — 22 WITH COORDINATES; CLOSED CASES switched cleanly and loaded 19 closed cases

Test Case Title: LE workflow actions closure controls and map link
Description: Verify reachable incident-detail actions without completing irreversible workflow changes
Test Steps: 1) Log in as LE 2) Open /lei 3) Inspect selected incident detail 4) Click DISPATCH UNIT and cancel 5) Click Publish to Community Feed 6) Click Fuzz Location for Privacy 7) Click CLOSE CASE and cancel 8) Inspect OPEN IN MAPS href
Expected Result: Action buttons open clear confirmation flows disclosure toggles behave predictably and map link is valid
Actual Result: DISPATCH UNIT opened a confirmation modal; CLOSE CASE opened a confirmation modal; Publish toggled on and enabled Fuzz which also toggled on; OPEN IN MAPS exposed a valid Google Maps coordinate URL

Description: Verify the first screen clearly communicates product identity purpose and primary actions
Test Steps: 1) Open http://localhost:5174/ 2) Review branding headings support text and primary CTAs
Expected Result: Product identity secure sign-in purpose and primary entry actions are obvious on first load
Actual Result: Login page showed SafeSignal branding secure-access messaging sign-in heading and clear LOGIN / APPLY ACCESS actions

Description: Verify labeled inputs password visibility toggle and clear sign-in CTA are present and usable
Test Steps: 1) Review email and password labels 2) Enter demo credentials 3) Toggle Show password
Expected Result: Inputs are labeled password can be revealed or hidden and the sign-in CTA is usable
Actual Result: Email and password fields were labeled Show password toggled to Hide password and revealed the password and the SIGN IN button was usable

Description: Verify empty and malformed submissions show actionable feedback
Test Steps: 1) Click SIGN IN with empty fields 2) Enter invalid-email and 123 3) Click SIGN IN again
Expected Result: Required-field and format or length validation messages appear and explain what to fix
Actual Result: Empty submit showed Email is required and Password is required; malformed submit showed Invalid email address and Minimum 6 characters

Description: Verify invalid credentials are rejected
Test Steps: 1) Enter wrong@example.com and wrongpass 2) Submit the form 3) Wait for server response
Expected Result: Access is denied and an error message is shown
Actual Result: Login stayed on /login and displayed Invalid credentials

Description: Verify visible demo credentials successfully authenticate and redirect to the dashboard
Test Steps: 1) Enter moderator@safesignal.com and password123 2) Submit the form 3) Confirm redirect and authenticated layout
Expected Result: Successful login redirects into the moderator dashboard
Actual Result: Login succeeded and redirected to / with moderator sidebar navigation visible

Description: Verify the access request tab accepts a valid request and returns success feedback
Test Steps: 1) Switch to APPLY ACCESS 2) Enter qa.moderator.20260428 / qa.moderator.20260428@example.com / SecurePass123! with role Moderator 3) Submit request 4) Attempt login with the new credentials
Expected Result: Valid request is accepted with success feedback and remains pending approval
Actual Result: Flow showed REQUEST SUBMITTED; later login with the new credentials was blocked with Account pending admin approval

Test Case Title: Protected route redirect while logged out
Description: Verify protected routes redirect safely when unauthenticated
Test Steps: 1) While logged out open / /reports /users /settings /admin /lei and /data-analysis-center 2) Observe resulting route each time
Expected Result: Protected routes do not render protected content and redirect to login
Actual Result: Each tested protected route redirected to /login and no protected content rendered while logged out

Description: Verify moderator-appropriate navigation is visible and restricted navigation is hidden
Test Steps: 1) Log in as moderator 2) Review sidebar navigation 3) Check for admin and LE links
Expected Result: Moderator nav items are visible and restricted admin and LE nav items are hidden
Actual Result: Dashboard Data Analysis Center Reports Users and Settings were visible; no Admin or LE nav links were shown

Description: Verify moderator access to /admin and /lei is denied safely
Test Steps: 1) While logged in as moderator open /admin 2) Open /lei 3) Observe resulting route and screen
Expected Result: Restricted routes are blocked without exposing restricted screens
Actual Result: Both /admin and /lei redirected back to / and the restricted screens were not exposed

Description: Verify main moderator pages can be opened after login
Test Steps: 1) Log in as moderator 2) Open / /reports /users /settings and /data-analysis-center 3) Check each page for basic render
Expected Result: Main moderator pages load and remain reachable after login
Actual Result: All target moderator pages were reachable during the authenticated session

Description: Verify report totals shown across main moderator screens are internally consistent
Test Steps: 1) Log in as moderator 2) Review dashboard report totals and recent activity 3) Open /reports and review ALL REPORTS count
Expected Result: Report counts across dashboard and reports views are consistent for the same environment
Actual Result: Dashboard showed 47 total reports with recent report activity while Reports showed 0 reports under ALL REPORTS

Description: Verify the dashboard operational map renders without visible failure
Test Steps: 1) Log in as moderator 2) Open dashboard 3) Inspect map panel
Expected Result: Dashboard map renders or degrades gracefully without explicit failure state
Actual Result: Dashboard visibly showed Failed to load Google Maps

Description: Verify a newly requested account is represented consistently across login and user management flows
Test Steps: 1) Submit a new access request 2) Attempt login with the new account 3) Open Users Management and inspect the new account status
Expected Result: A pending account remains pending everywhere until approved
Actual Result: Login blocked the new account as pending admin approval but Users Management listed the same new account as ACTIVE

Description: Verify logout clears the session and protected pages are no longer accessible
Test Steps: 1) Click Logout from authenticated view 2) Confirm redirect to login 3) Open /reports after logout
Expected Result: User is logged out redirected to login and protected routes are blocked again
Actual Result: Logout returned to /login and reopening /reports redirected back to /login

Description: Verify the tested flows do not produce uncaught runtime or resource errors
Test Steps: 1) Execute login navigation denial redirect and logout flows 2) Review preserved console messages in DevTools
Expected Result: No uncaught runtime or resource errors occur during the tested flows
Actual Result: Console contained repeated Failed to load resource: net::ERR_PROXY_CONNECTION_FAILED errors along with non-blocking warnings
